title: 10 best herbal teas for flu
intro_desc: Herbal teas for flu are infusions made from various plants and spices, which can help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with the flu virus. These teas can soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and alleviate body aches. Examples of herbal teas that can help with flu include chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and echinacea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat, while ginger tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. Peppermint tea can help clear nasal congestion and soothe a sore throat, and echinacea tea can help boost the immune system. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from their flu symptoms, allowing them to rest and recover more comfortably. This can lead to a quicker recovery time and a reduction in the severity of symptoms, improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with flu because it has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms and potentially shorten the duration of the illness. The herb contains compounds such as lactones and flavonoids, which are known to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, yarrow tea can help to promote perspiration, which can aid in the body's natural ability to eliminate toxins and reduce fever. Its soothing and calming effects can also help to alleviate symptoms such as cough, sore throat, and congestion, providing relief and comfort during a flu episode.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of flu. This is mainly due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antipyretic: Yarrow tea can help reduce fever associated with the flu, thanks to its antipyretic properties. The active constituents responsible for this are alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties help fight off the virus causing the flu. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and lactones.
Diaphoretic: Yarrow tea promotes sweating, which helps eliminate toxins from the body, aiding in the recovery from the flu. This is mainly due to the presence of lactones and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: Yarrow tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the flu virus. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tea for flu due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, the flowers of the yarrow plant are used in tea to alleviate symptoms of flu, such as fever and cough, by promoting sweating and easing congestion.
Stems: Containing therapeutic compounds, the stems of the yarrow plant are used in tea to help reduce inflammation, soothe sore throats, and boost the immune system during flu.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, the seeds are used in yarrow tea to provide antispasmodic and diaphoretic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of flu, such as cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the yarrow in a cup or teapot.
Steep the yarrow in hot water for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain out the yarrow leaves and flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Sip the warm yarrow tea, optionally adding honey or lemon for flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with flu because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a gel when mixed with fluid. This gel-like consistency coats the throat and soothes irritation, reducing coughing and soreness. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. The tea can also help alleviate fever and promote hydration, both of which are crucial for recovery from the flu. Furthermore, marshmallow tea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which may help the body fight off the flu virus and prevent secondary infections.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea is rich in mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, thus alleviating common flu symptoms.
Expectorant: The tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reduce congestion caused by the flu virus.

Antitussive: Marshmallow tea contains constituents, such as flavonoids and triterpene saponins, that help soothe coughs and reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits associated with the flu.
Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, which may help inhibit the growth of flu-causing viruses and bacteria, potentially shortening the duration of the illness.
Immune-Boosting: The tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamins, such as vitamin A and C, which support the immune system, helping the body fight off the flu virus more effectively.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals, leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, helping to alleviate flu symptoms.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, flowers are used to soothe a sore throat, reduce fever, and boost the immune system during flu.
Stems: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, stems are utilized to ease body aches and pains associated with the flu.
Roots: Known for their antiviral properties, roots are used to help shorten the duration of the flu and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Bark: With a high concentration of tannins, bark is used for its astringent properties, helping to dry up a runny nose and relieve cough.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water, 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries, 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea root, and 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid plant material.
Add honey to taste while the tea is still warm, stirring until it is fully dissolved.
Drink the tea up to three times a day for relief of flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with flu because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of the flu. Wormwood contains compounds such as artemisinin and thujone, which have been shown to have antiviral effects against certain types of flu viruses. Additionally, wormwood tea can help stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can help boost the immune system and speed up recovery time. The warm tea can also help soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion. Drinking wormwood tea can provide a natural and effective way to help manage flu symptoms and support overall health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Wormwood tea contains compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antiviral properties, potentially helping to inhibit the replication of the flu virus.
Anti-inflammatory property: Wormwood tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of the flu, such as fever, cough, and sore throat.
Immune-boosting property: Wormwood tea contains antioxidants and other compounds that can help support the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the flu virus.
Fever-reducing property: Wormwood tea has been traditionally used as a fever reducer, which can help manage one of the common symptoms of the flu.
Expectorant property: Wormwood tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and potentially reducing the duration of the flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood tea made from the leaves is often used to alleviate flu symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of wormwood contain potent compounds, making them a popular addition to medicinal tea for the flu, as they can help stimulate sweating and boost the immune system.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are rich in essential oils and have been used in teas to help soothe digestive issues often associated with the flu, such as nausea and abdominal pain.
Bark: The bark of the wormwood plant is known for its antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a valuable addition to medicinal tea for treating and preventing the flu.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried wormwood herb; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, strain the tea into a cup, and discard the used herb.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for added flavor and potential immune-boosting benefits.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for soothing relief of flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with flu because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms and boost the immune system. The warm beverage can soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and relieve coughing. Additionally, cinnamon has been found to have antiviral properties, which may help to shorten the duration of the flu. Furthermore, the drink can help to rehydrate the body, which is essential when fighting off an illness. Overall, cinnamon tea can provide much-needed relief and support the body's natural defenses during flu season.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Cinnamon tea may help fight off the flu virus due to its antiviral properties, attributed to compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon tea can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like coughing and sore throat, thanks to its anti-inflammatory compounds, including cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid.
Immune-boosting properties: Cinnamon tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the flu virus, due to its rich content of antioxidants and polyphenols.

Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon tea can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by the flu virus, as it contains powerful antioxidants like cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and various polyphenols.

Diaphoretic properties: Cinnamon tea can promote sweating, which can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever, a common symptom of the flu, due to its diaphoretic effect, attributed to compounds like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tea plant has antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing the severity of flu symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon tea plant contain compounds that help boost the immune system, providing relief from flu-related symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the cinnamon tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate muscle aches and pains associated with the flu.
Stems: The stems of the cinnamon tea plant contain essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, aiding in the prevention of the spread of the flu virus.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 cinnamon stick (3-4 inches long) and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Optional: Add 1 teaspoon of honey or lemon juice for added flavor and vitamin C.
Strain the tea to remove cinnamon pieces.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe a sore throat and alleviate flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with flu because it is made from a blend of herbs known for their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties. The tea's natural ingredients, such as echinacea, elderberry, and peppermint, work together to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Additionally, the warm liquid can help to ease coughing and promote relaxation, allowing the body to rest and recover. By providing symptomatic relief and supporting the immune system, Eureka tea can be a helpful addition to a flu-fighting routine.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Eureka tea contains compounds such as catechins and theaflavins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses, including influenza viruses, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the flu.

Antioxidant property: The high antioxidant content of Eureka tea, mainly due to its polyphenol content, helps protect cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can be elevated during flu infections.

Anti-inflammatory property: Eureka tea has anti-inflammatory properties, thanks to compounds like epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and theaflavins, which can help alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract and reduce symptoms associated with the flu.

Immunomodulatory property: Eureka tea can help regulate and enhance the immune response, making it more effective at fighting off infections. This is due to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including polysaccharides and flavonoids.
Decongestant property: The caffeine content in Eureka tea can help alleviate nasal congestion by acting as a mild stimulant, promoting the clearing of airways and improving breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and vitamins, leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tea for flu due to their immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Containing various beneficial compounds, flowers are used in medicinal tea for flu to help alleviate symptoms, such as fever and sore throat, and to support overall respiratory health.
Seeds: High in essential oils and fatty acids, seeds are used in medicinal tea for flu to provide antiviral and expectorant benefits, helping to clear congestion and soothe coughs.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea root, 1 tablespoon of dried elderberry, and 2 cups of water, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer the herbs in water for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then stir in honey and lemon juice to taste.
Serve the herbal Eureka tea hot, and enjoy up to three cups daily for flu relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with flu because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which can help to fight off the virus and prevent further infection. The herbal tea can also help to soothe a sore throat, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, providing relief and comfort during a flu episode. Its natural ingredients make it a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter flu medications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains compounds such as chlorogenic acid and luteolin, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, making it effective in reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's active constituents, such as caffeic acid and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion, sore throat, and other flu-related symptoms.
Immune-boosting properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamin C, which support the immune system, helping the body fight off the flu virus more effectively.

Antipyretic properties: The tea's ability to reduce fever is attributed to its antipyretic constituents, such as luteolin and cichoric acid, providing relief from high temperatures associated with the flu.

Expectorant properties: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits, thanks to its expectorant compounds like chlorogenic acid and luteolin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating flu symptoms.
Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to boost the immune system and aid in fighting off the flu virus.
Roots: Packed with essential oils and antimicrobial compounds, making them useful for treating respiratory infections associated with the flu.
Seeds: High in vitamins and minerals, providing necessary nutrients to support the body's immune response during flu recovery.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Steep the herb in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, as needed for flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with flu because it contains several beneficial compounds, such as saponins, flavonoids, and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieve cough and congestion, and boost the immune system, making it easier to fight off the flu virus. Additionally, horsetail tea is a natural diuretic, which can help flush toxins out of the body and speed up recovery. Furthermore, horsetail tea is rich in minerals like silica, which can help strengthen the immune system and promote overall health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Horsetail tea contains compounds such as saponins and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses, including those that cause the flu.
Anti-inflammatory property: The silica and other antioxidants present in horsetail tea can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of the flu.
Diuretic property: Horsetail tea can help flush out toxins from the body, reducing the duration and severity of flu symptoms.
Expectorant property: Horsetail tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the risk of respiratory infections.
Immune-boosting property: Horsetail tea contains nutrients such as vitamin C, selenium, and zinc that support the immune system, helping the body fight off the flu virus.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of horsetail tea plant are rich in silica, which can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it an effective remedy for flu symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of horsetail tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and vitamins, which can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion, making it a popular choice for flu remedies.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of horsetail tea plant are a storehouse of nutrients, including minerals and vitamins, which can help strengthen the body's defenses and promote recovery from the flu.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the horsetail herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb pieces.
Drink the tea, up to 3 times a day, as needed for flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with flu because it contains powerful antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate symptoms and reduce the duration of the illness. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia and has been used for centuries by indigenous people to treat a variety of ailments. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to prevent the spread of the flu virus in the body. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea can help to soothe a sore throat, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a great natural remedy for the flu.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol and eucalyptol, which can help inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria that can cause flu.

Antioxidant properties: The high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids in the tea helps protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, boosting the immune system and aiding in flu recovery.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of compounds like cineole and linalool in the tea can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, alleviating symptoms associated with the flu.
Expectorant properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by the flu.
Immune-boosting properties: The tea's ability to stimulate the immune system, partly due to its high antioxidant content, can help the body fight off the flu virus more effectively.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have antiviral properties, making them effective in alleviating flu symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the plant contains tannins and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to soothe a sore throat and expel mucus.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off the flu virus and reduce its duration.

Seeds: The seeds of the plant contain essential oils and fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, helping to alleviate flu symptoms and strengthen the body's defenses.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 4 cups of water.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves to a teapot.
Once water is boiling, pour it over the leaves in the teapot and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into cups, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea throughout the day for relief of flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with flu because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. This means that it can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieve congestion, and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, licorice tea can help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the virus. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to alleviate symptoms by promoting perspiration and hydration, which can help to break a fever and replenish fluids lost due to illness.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Licorice tea helps loosen and thin mucus in the airways, making it easier to cough up and expel it from the body, which is important for reducing congestion and inflammation in the respiratory tract caused by the flu. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as sore throat and cough, which is important for reducing discomfort and promoting healing. This property is also due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Antiviral: Licorice tea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help prevent the replication of the flu virus in the body, thus reducing the severity and duration of the illness. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and coumarins.

Immune-boosting: Licorice tea helps stimulate the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the flu virus. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to increase the production of interferons, proteins that play a crucial role in the body's defense against viruses.

Demulcent: Licorice tea has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, forming a protective film that helps to relieve irritation and inflammation. This property is due to the high mucilage content of licorice root.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, the roots of the licorice tea plant have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing sore throats and relieving congestion.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, the leaves of the licorice tea plant help boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of flu, such as fever and fatigue.
Flowers: Packed with vitamin C and other essential nutrients, the flowers of the licorice tea plant aid in fighting off infections and reducing inflammation caused by the flu.

Seeds: Containing essential oils and compounds with antiviral and antibacterial properties, the seeds of the licorice tea plant help in preventing the spread of the flu virus and easing respiratory symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 8 cups of water and 1/4 cup of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey to taste, if desired, for added sweetness and soothing properties.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea daily for relief of flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with flu because it contains beneficial compounds that can help alleviate symptoms. The plant has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory infections. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Meanwhile, its antimicrobial properties can help fight off the virus causing the flu, while its expectorant properties can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs. Drinking English ivy tea can help soothe a sore throat, relieve congestion, and boost the immune system, providing much-needed relief during flu season.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: English ivy tea has antiviral properties that can help to inhibit the growth of the virus responsible for the flu, reducing the severity and duration of the illness. This is due to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral effects.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English ivy tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and congestion. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Expectorant properties: English ivy tea has expectorant properties that can help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. This is due to the presence of saponins, which have expectorant effects.

Antioxidant properties: English ivy tea has antioxidant properties that can help to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can be produced in larger quantities during an infection. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects.

Immune-boosting properties: English ivy tea has immune-boosting properties that can help to strengthen the body's defenses against the flu virus. This is due to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to have immune-boosting effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for flu due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy tea plant contain compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of the flu, such as congestion and cough.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy tea plant are used in medicinal teas for their expectorant and antispasmodic properties, which can help clear mucus and soothe a sore throat.
Flowers: The flowers of the English ivy tea plant are used in teas for their antioxidant properties, which can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh English ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a pot, then add 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal properties of the leaves.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink 2-3 times a day for relief of flu symptoms.
